title: Replace regex-only chat filter with structured message classification
description:
Upgrade the chat/system-message filter from a regex firewall to structured classification with `messageKind` field. Per the Mineflayer audit, the current `SYSTEM_MESSAGE_PATTERNS` array is brittle — any server phrasing change can leak noise into the LLM pipeline.

Implementation:
1. Add a `messageKind` classification emitted with every chat event:
   - `player_chat` — message from another player
   - `server_info` — server announcement (join/leave, time set, gamemode change)
   - `server_command_echo` — server echo of a command (teleport, give, clear)
   - `server_error` — permission denied, unknown command, etc.
   - `death_message` — player/mob death announcement
   - `advancement` — advancement earned announcement
2. Classification strategy:
   - First: check `username` — empty/null → server message (reliable)
   - Second: check JSON message structure if available (Mineflayer 4.x exposes `json` property)
   - Third: regex patterns as fallback only
3. Add `messageKind` to `ChatEvent` in `WorldEvents.cs`:
   - `ChatEvent(string Username, string Message, string? MessageKind, int OnlinePlayers, Position? PlayerPos, DateTimeOffset Timestamp)`
4. Forward `messageKind` to C# so `AgentBackgroundService` can filter at the C# level:
   - Only `player_chat` and `server_command_echo` reach the LLM
   - `server_info` and `death_message` logged but not forwarded
5. Keep existing regex patterns as the fallback tier, not the primary classifier.

Acceptance:
- New server message types are less likely to leak through.
- C# side can distinguish chat sources without re-parsing text.
- No regression: existing filtered messages stay filtered.

Refs: `msa_planning_mineflayer_audit_20260621_1437.md` Finding 3; `mineflayer-adapter-research.md` related section
